p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, stable posture.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remained consistent throughout the sequence; no significant growth or decline observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Stable. No signs of chlorosis or wilting.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, two primary leaves, two emerging smaller leaves.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Maintained structural integrity; leaf orientation remains consistent with the light source.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The leaf proximal to the rabbit shows slight marginal browning that has stabilized since T-4.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Stressed. Marginal necrosis on the larger leaf suggests past moisture or nutrient imbalance.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot, small seedling near rim.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Remained static; no new leaf development observed over the 5-day period.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Dormant/Slow-growth. No signs of distress, but minimal metabolic activity.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Soil moisture appears consistent; white perlite/mineral deposits are present in the black pots.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no debris or fungal growth detected.

temporal_deltas.t_minus_4_to_t_minus_3: Significant change in camera angle/perspective; soil surface in p3/p4 shows addition of white mineral/perlite top-dressing.
temporal_deltas.t_minus_3_to_t_minus_2: Minor shift in perlite distribution; plant positions remain fixed.
temporal_deltas.t_minus_2_to_current: Stability maintained; no further physical changes to plant morphology.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of 'Managed Stasis'. The plants are not actively growing but are maintaining their current physiological state. The Pothos (p3) is the most sensitive element, requiring monitoring of the necrotic leaf margins.
vision_report.anomalies: The sudden appearance of white mineral/perlite particles between T-4 and T-3 indicates human intervention (top-dressing) which was not present in the earliest image.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is currently stable. The transition from the earliest image to the current state shows a deliberate addition of soil amendments (perlite) to the black pots. The plants are exhibiting signs of acclimatization to the indoor environment, with no acute signs of decline or rapid growth.
